conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Jean-Christophe Goujon (36, based in Port-la-Nouvelle, southern France) is a director/operations specialist in the HPA (outdoor hospitality/camping) sector with 15+ years of experience, including his most recent role as Director at Naïa Village (Barcarès, ~500 mobile homes), which he left in early January 2026. He is currently registered with France Travail while simultaneously launching an AI/data consulting offer targeting HPA operators (dashboards, forecasts, automation, KPIs, processes) and developing several parallel entrepreneurial projects. He is also a certified RNCP professional coach operating under the brand Mind'Up – Coaching, Formation & Consulting, working primarily with executives and mid-level managers.

**Personal context**

Jean-Christophe has three children: Leandro, Lanaï, and Lino (Lino attends crèche on a shared alternating schedule with his partner Pauline). He is based in Port-la-Nouvelle (Aude, near Perpignan) and enjoys family outdoor activities including hiking and skiing in the Pyrénées. He supports Pauline Roland on comics-related business matters (copyright, contracts, VAT/royalties). He has contacts including school contacts Isabelle Letellier and Adeline Gourdon.

**Top of mind**

Jean-Christophe is actively running his coaching practice, having recently deployed a custom AI coaching assistant ("CoachAssist Pro") and conducted live sessions with a coachee named Catherine (a commercial team manager navigating managerial isolation and role transition). He is also preparing for a vide-grenier on April 5th, for which he has developed a full printed stand document with dual vendor/customer cover pages. His Mercedes GLC (recently serviced with full transmission work at Monopole Perpignan) may be positioned for sale with the invoice as a commercial asset.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

**Coaching practice:** Jean-Christophe built a full ChatGPT-based coaching agent with multi-framework support (systemic, TA, NLP, CBT, CNV, solution-focused, narrative, positive psychology), covering five defined missions. He is using it actively in the field with structured session prep documents, branching question arborescences, and post-session analysis. His preferred coaching deliverables are ready-to-use, theoretically grounded, and formatted for immediate field use.

**BarCoins (SaaS gamification platform):** An active development project — a bar loyalty and gamification WebSaaS using quiz and blind test gameplay as the primary coin/reward mechanism, targeting HoReCa and potentially HPA/camping operators. Jean-Christophe has worked on UI redesign (warmer amber/dark palette, Bebas Neue typography, CSS animations), coin economy rebalancing (new GOLD tier at 4,000 coins, centralized coins.config.js), and competitive analysis vs. ThisisBlindTest. He uses OpenClaw (Telegram bot: jcduss35_bot) + Claude Code on WSL/Ubuntu as his development stack, with the Anthropic API for autonomous agent tasks.

**My Naïa Event Excel tool:** Built a professional 10-tab Excel workbook for an event services department within a camping operation, covering dashboard, pipeline, revenue breakdown by venue (Naïa Village, Salle Cinéma, Salle Ado, Complexe 66, Restaurant 66, Le Dauphin, Le WR), charges, KPIs, P&L, cash flow, and charts. Color conventions: blue = manual input, black = formulas, green = cross-sheet links.

**Vehicle and financial matters:** Navigated a complex financial restructuring involving vehicle sales (Mercedes GLC 300e), credit consolidation (regroupement de crédit), and ARE unemployment benefits (since January 2026). Pursued ARCE eligibility analysis given an existing inactive micro-enterprise (SIREN 534483748, coaching, created May 2023). Also managing a real estate sale situation involving a mandataire and wire transfer coordination.

**Technical stack and AI infrastructure:** Set up Claude Code on WSL/Ubuntu across two PCs; uses OpenClaw with main agent + subagent architecture; explored Claude Code Remote Control for mobile access; has a Telegram bot integration. Recommended architecture: OpenClaw for orchestration/Telegram interface, Anthropic API direct calls for autonomous file/code construction, Claude Code for interactive development.

**Job search and professional positioning:** Applied to HPA director roles (CIELA VILLAGE at La Croix du Sud/Barcarès, then Gers) while managing France Travail compliance. Developed differentiated application strategy positioning himself as a senior HPA operator with AI/data capabilities rather than a standard applicant. Also contacted Adrien Fuchs (adrienfuchs@ecg.camp) via LinkedIn and email.

**Legal/employment dispute:** Contested dismissal from Naïa Village (gross misconduct allegation), prepared documentation for employment tribunal (prud'hommes) and a whistleblowing report to labor inspection (inspection du travail) covering multiple alleged violations by former employer Sébastien Guillaudeux.

*Earlier context*

**HPA AI consulting vision:** Jean-Christophe defined his core entrepreneurial offer as AI/data tools for HPA operators — virtual receptionists/voicebots for reception, staff scheduling automation (VBA, rotating days off, optimized labor costs), KPI dashboards, and forecasting tools. He frames this as an "infrastructure d'augmentation entrepreneuriale" and wants to intervene only for validations, with agents handling construction autonomously.

**Chogan (MLM perfumes/cosmetics):** Explored as a parallel income stream, targeting his HPA industry network. Strategy focused on positioning as a business opportunity rather than product sales, leveraging his camping operations management background.

**Tourism domain project:** 10 hectares, 5 themed zones, tiny houses/small chalets under 45m², targeting a budget of ~700k–1M€ with tax optimization and financing strategies.

**F&B concepts:** "Bar à Sauce" and "Défi Flamme du 66" — both with animation, character, and upsell strategy components.

**Excel/financial tools:** Built a comprehensive camping KPI dashboard (six tabs: Dashboard, Recettes, Charges, Analyse Services, Paramètres) using openpyxl/Python, with French accounting conventions (classe 6xx/7xx), departmental cost breakdowns, and seasonal projections.

**Childcare scheduling:** Created an alternating weekly crèche schedule (Semaine A/B) for Lino with Pauline, including Word/PDF documents and an 80-event ICS calendar file starting March 2026.

*Long-term background*

Jean-Christophe has deep operational expertise in HPA — occupancy rates, RevPAR, forecast vs. actual, revenue by segment, F&B concepts, seasonal ramp-up, staff management, and HPA convention compliance. He has a strong professional network in the Barcarès/Pyrénées-Orientales area and familiarity with major tour operators (VacanceSelect, Roan, Marvilla, Tohapi, Eurocamp). His long-term ambitions include financial independence, becoming an AI expert with daily AI news integration, and building signature tourism, F&B, and SaaS tool projects. Key self-identified vigilance points: multi-project dispersion, perfectionism/impatience, premature automation tendency, and need for calibrated direct communication in professional contexts.

---

**Other instructions**

- Always deliver ready-to-use outputs: complete emails, documents, prompts, scripts, and formatted deliverables — not outlines or suggestions requiring further work.
- Jean-Christophe uses French Excel syntax (SOMMEPROD, SOMME, SUMIF equivalents in French) — always use French formula syntax for spreadsheet work.
- Professional deliverables should have proper layout, graphics, and structure — "bankable" quality with visual formatting (colored banners, tables, charts) suitable for presentation or field use.
- He values proactive challenge to his approach, not just execution — flag risks, blind spots, or better framings when relevant.
- Communication style: direct, structured, result-oriented. Use tables, ranked options, and prioritized action steps. Avoid padding.
- For coaching work: outputs should include theoretical grounding, branching question arborescences, and documents formatted for immediate session use.
- For technical/AI work: his stack is WSL/Ubuntu, VS Code, OpenClaw (jcduss35_bot), Claude Code, Anthropic API. Recommend SSH + VS Code Remote SSH for remote access.
- Legal and HR matters require caution — flag risks proactively, especially around France Travail compliance, HPA convention, and employment law.

project_memories.019c4c12-7e7e-770b-b4e6-8b9246122aed:
**Purpose & context**

Jean-Christophe is pursuing a multi-faceted strategy toward achieving independence by age 40, operating across four interconnected professional domains. As a 37-year-old former director, he's simultaneously managing legal proceedings, building consulting services, pursuing executive opportunities, and developing specialized business tools. His approach reflects both immediate financial needs and longer-term strategic positioning in business consulting and management roles.

The work spans legal advocacy (defending his professional reputation and securing owed compensation), entrepreneurial ventures (AI-focused consulting for SMEs and hospitality), corporate strategy (targeting executive positions with major hospitality groups), and product development (management tools for creative professionals). Jean-Christophe has explicitly requested comprehensive strategic partnership, emphasizing the importance of understanding how these initiatives interconnect within his broader independence goals.

**Current state**

Jean-Christophe is actively managing legal proceedings regarding his termination from Nai'a Village, involving contested dismissal procedures, unpaid contractual bonuses, and ongoing regulatory complaints. Simultaneously, he's developing his consulting business focused on AI implementation for SMEs and hospitality businesses using his "CAP" methodology (Clarity-Automation-Performance), with structured service packages ranging from audits to specialized tools.

He's also pursuing executive opportunities with Camping Paradis/Maeva group following their recent acquisition, including developing proposals for operational improvements and franchise management systems. Additionally, he's creating specialized management tools for comic book authors and creators, covering accounting, tax compliance, and rights management.

**Key learnings & principles**

Jean-Christophe values direct, pragmatic assistance that challenges his ideas rather than providing uncritical validation. He emphasizes the importance of comprehensive context understanding, describing his project documentation as his "current active memory" and requesting that strategic partners maintain perfect knowledge of interconnections between his various initiatives.

His CAP methodology (Clarity-Automation-Performance) represents a structured approach to AI implementation consulting, suggesting he's developed systematic frameworks for business transformation work.

**Approach & patterns**

Jean-Christophe maintains comprehensive documentation of his strategic planning and project development, using detailed conversation histories and materials to track progress across multiple initiatives. He seeks strategic partnerships where collaborators have full visibility into his multi-faceted professional situation and can provide informed guidance across all active projects simultaneously.

His approach involves parallel development across legal, consulting, corporate, and product development tracks, suggesting comfort with managing complex, interconnected professional portfolios rather than sequential project focus.
project_memories.019c8f9e-ff8c-75a7-89a9-362a7f7ccff5:
**Purpose & context**

Jean-Christophe Goujon (JC), solo founder of BarCoins, is building a B2B SaaS PWA gamification platform that transforms bars into competitive social arenas through virtual currency mechanics, games, and loyalty systems. Based in Port-la-Nouvelle (Aude, Occitanie). Target sectors: CHR (bars, restaurants) and HPA (holiday parks/camping). Beta launch target: July 2026 with 5 Founding Partner bars in Perpignan. Commercial launch: October 2026.

**Full founder identity:** Jean-Christophe Goujon (JC), 16 rue des Anciens Chantiers, 11210 Port-la-Nouvelle (Aude, Occitanie). **RÈGLE ABSOLUE : toujours vérifier la mémoire avant de générer tout document contenant des noms ou coordonnées.**

**Key people:**
- **Yannick Lance** — potential co-founding partner, strong local network in département 66, event management background, Notion/Excel skills. NDA + exploratory mission signed. 30–60 day evaluation period.
- **Sabrina Pagès** — network prescriber, CHR connections via Guasch family network. NDA + prescription agreement signed.
- **Arthur / Julien** — developer candidates to contact.

**Background:** JC validated the BarCoins concept physically in his own hospitality establishments using printed currency, proving customers stayed longer and consumed more. BarCoins is the digital productization of that proven mechanic. He has deep hospitality sector experience (HPA/camping operations management).

---

**Current state**

**SASU BarCoins:** In process of registration (not yet officially created). Immediate actions: SASU creation + INPI filing + Qonto account opening. ACRE to be requested at creation. Expert-comptable to be mandated.

**Financing:** Dependent on vehicle sale (~27,000€ sale, ~10K repurchase, net capital ~14,000€ after account reset). Pursuing Initiative Pays Catalan (prêt d'honneur, target 15,000€) and Créalia Occitanie (innovation loan, target 20,000€). Total amorçage estimated 12,965€ of which 9,000€ is dev. No salary from SASU until M13 (target 30% monthly revenue, capped at 5,000€ net — to be validated by accountant). ARE must not be touched under any circumstances.

**Development:** Stack: Next.js, Prisma, Stripe, Spotify/Deezer API, Tailwind, PostgreSQL. Overall project advancement: ~38%. Active dev corrections to apply:
- ERR-01: Pricing renamed STARTER/STANDARD/PREMIUM (replaces old ESSENTIEL/FULL/ENTERPRISE in Prisma + Stripe)
- ERR-02: `wheelEnabled=false` feature flag for entire beta
- ERR-03: Blind test = Spotify + Deezer API integration
- ERR-04: Onboarding 3 phases = `OnboardingPhase` enum + `requirePhase()` middleware
- New MVP components: SP-01 (music API), HS-01 (health score), GM-01 (gérant pressé), OB-01 (onboarding), WF-01 (wheel flag)

**Legal:** ANJ mandate before end of March 2026 (1,500€). Total legal budget: 3,500€ (ANJ 1,500€ + CGU 800€ + règlement jeux 1,200€). Loi Évin risk mitigation: replace "virtual currency / earn coins by consuming" language with "loyalty points / visit validated by manager" framing; never display monetary equivalence publicly; reposition manager as active point attributor. Avoid "token" (MiCA/crypto risk). Lawyer consultation recommended before commercial launch (~400–600€, consumer law + public health specialty). `wheelEnabled=false` for entire beta — Roue activation post-ANJ only. Plan B Roue to be documented.

**OpenClaw:** Operational at localhost:18789. 6 active agents: main, dev, legal, product, cfo, growth. Agent dev on claude-sonnet, others on gpt-4.1. Telegram bot: jcduss35_bot. AI budget: ~80$/month OpenAI + ~10$/month Anthropic.

**Investor score:** Currently 58/100, target 70–72 at October 2026 launch, potential 78–80 at M+9 with strong beta results.

---

**On the horizon**

- Confirm 5 Founding Partners orally; freeze beta scope in writing
- Contact dev candidates (Arthur/Julien)
- Contact Sabrina (Guasch network activation)
- Initiative Perpignan (prêt d'honneur) contact
- BPI France amorçage dossier
- NDA template + Soleau envelope
- Contrat-cadre approach for HPA groups (priority targets: Sandaya, Huttopia, Villages Clubs du Soleil)
- Brasserie prescriptors (Heineken/Kronenbourg/Carlsberg)
- MyBeers franchise network (45 bars, quiz/blind test animation) — framework contract approach at M9
- Beta protocol documentation: baseline CA before/after + filmed manager testimonial
- Post-beta pricing increases: Standard → 169€, Premium → 299–349€
- V1 post-beta à la carte options: Roue +29€, Multi-site +49€/site, Animateur avancé +19€, Data export +39€, Branding blanc +49€, Support prioritaire +29€, Championnat prioritaire +19€
- 4 packages post-beta: Bar Soirée 169€, HPA/Camping 319€, Hôtel/Restaurant 199€, Découverte Beta 89€ (15j gratuits)
- YouTube transcription pipeline for Claude/Claude Code
- Notion environment setup (JC open to adoption if Claude builds it)

---

**Key learnings & principles**

**Product:**
- BarCoins is the only platform operating across all 3 circles simultaneously: animation + fidélité + gamification économique. Competitors (Amusoire, Cotton Quiz, Hey Pongo, ZeroSix, Kadow) each cover only 1–2.
- Blind test is stakeable (Play coins misable) = absolute market differentiator vs all competitors. Do not try to match Amusoire on content volume — win on economic mechanic.
- The Roue was created to solve a specific problem: Fidélité coins purchased with real money need a high-perceived-value redemption mechanic that captures BarCoins margin without creating a net loss for the bar.
- FOMO mechanic is core: the leaderboard never stops — players lose positions while absent (Ractus Poker model).
- HPA segment: 70–80% of camping bars operate under management contracts, not direct ownership → referral network, not direct acquisition target. Target groups with directly operated bars for framework contracts.
- Claude Code must never independently set business values. All unvalidated values use `config/business-rules.ts` with `TODO_VALIDER_JC` placeholders.

**Legal:**
- The coins-per-consumption mechanic is legally defensible and already practiced at national scale (3 Brasseurs, Hippopotamus, Au Bureau). Solution is semantic + documentary, not structural.
- Quiz and blind test = skill-based competitions, not lotteries. Critical ANJ distinction.
- Leaderboard rewards (soirée/hebdo/mensuel) are entirely at the bar's expense — internal consumption vouchers, zero cash flow, zero BarCoins invoice deduction. "Bon remis" dashboard validation = analytics only, not accounting.

**Go-to-market:**
- Primary acquisition: direct business networking, brasserie prescriptors, qualified terrain demos — not cold email.
- Commercial ROI speech: 8 engaged clients/evening → packs collected 85€, lots reimbursed to bar, net bar cost = 0€. Additional revenue ×2.5 vs without BarCoins → +180€ revenue → +126€ gross margin (70%). Subscription per evening = 2.97€. Net gain = +123€/evening. Profitable from the first evening.
- Discounts → replace with exclusivity positioning.

**NotebookLM:** Source document tone directly determines podcast output tone. Write source in target voice (narrative, oral, dialogued) not as a reference document.

---

**Approach & patterns**

- **Working style:** Direct, action-oriented, no preamble, no excessive caveats. Pushes back immediately when Claude is wrong or overly cautious. Prefers Claude to act as a senior entrepreneurial partner anticipating what hasn't been thought of, not just answering questions.
- **Language:** French for all project work.
- **Document generation rule:** Always check memory before generating any document containing names or personal details.
- **Business value rule:** Claude Code never sets business values autonomously. All values require JC validation.
- **Financial tracking:** Excel cockpit updated weekly on Fridays. OpenClaw dashboard (localhost:18789) for daily operational piloting.
- **Storage:** OneDrive + Google Drive. Claude Code via WSL terminal.
- **Bible methodology:** Ordered linear progression through all 8 parts (P1→P8), each delivered in PDF + DOCX + Markdown before moving to next.
- **OpenClaw workflow:** Agents produce domain documents → Claude synthesizes into master action plan (who/when/what/where/how/cost/duration format).

---

**Product reference (validated March 2026)**

**Coin economy:**
- 1 BarCoin = 0.50€ perceived value
- Multiplier tiers: ×1 / ×1.5 / ×2 / ×3 / ×4 (by consumption level)
- Play coins: earned through consumption, stakeable in PvP
- Fidélité coins: purchased directly, never stakeable
- Boost ×2 rule: `effectiveMultiplier = min(plan.maxMultiplier × 2, 6)`. Starter(×2)+Boost=×4, Standard(×3)+Boost=×6, Premium(×4)+Boost=×6 (capped). Server-side verification only.

**Packs Fidélité V2:**
- Découverte 5€ = 15c + 1 spin (cost/coin: 0.333€)
- Standard 10€ = 35c + 2 spins (cost/coin: 0.286€)
- Premium 20€ = 90c + 4 spins + badge + 20c Play offered (cost/coin: 0.222€)
- Beta perceived value (wheelEnabled=false): Découverte 7.50€, Standard 17.50€, Premium 45€
- Post-ANJ perceived value: Découverte ~10.25€, Standard ~23€, Premium ~56€
- Display spins as "available after Roue launch" during beta

**5 Fidélité usages (equal UI parity):** Roue (15c/spin), Réductions note (10c=0.50€, max 30% of bill, absolute max 50€), Statuts soirée (30–200c), Tournois premium (10c), Boost ×2 (15c/1h)

**Rewards catalog:**
- Shot=30c, Bière=40c, Bière qualité=50c, Cocktail=90c, Premium=140c, Planche=150c
- Fidélité: 10c=0.50€ reduction, max 30% of bill
- Discount caps: 10%→max 10€, 20%→max 20€, absolute 50€/bill

**Statuts Soirée:**
- VIP Écran: 30c, golden pseudo 2h, 100% margin
- Maître du Quiz: 50c, theme choice, 100% margin
- Double ou Rien: 60c, ×2 coins 2h, multi-holders allowed, 100% margin
- DJ For a Moment: 40c, 3 music tracks, 100% margin
- Mon Cocktail: 80c, cocktail in their name 1h, ~10€ cost
- Le Legend: 200c, everything included + ×2 3h, 1 per evening only
- Rule: 1 active simultaneously except Double ou Rien. 60s cancellation window.

**Leaderboards & championships:**
- Soirée / Hebdo / Mensuel / Annuel
- Soirée reward: conso 8€ (bar's expense)
- Hebdo rewards: vouchers 15/8/4€ (bar's expense)
- Mensuel rewards: vouchers 50/25/10€ (bar's expense)
- Annuel rewards: all-inclusive trip / voucher 200€ / 100€ (BarCoins expense, financed from PvP/tournament commissions)
- Championship: local (M1–10) / régional (M11) / finale (M12). Bar = most accumulated coins.

**Roue de la Fortune:**
- 10,000 cases. `wheelEnabled=false` during beta.
- Average cost/spin: 2.091€ (jackpot included)
- Average revenue/spin (realistic mix 40% Découverte / 40% Standard / 20% Premium): 4.38€
- Real margin: ~52.5% (not 44.9%). Pessimistic (100% Premium): 37.2%. Optimistic (100% Découverte): 58.2%.
- Jackpot provision: 0.30€/spin
- Physical prizes reimbursed to bar via monthly invoice deduction
- Roue margin projections: 30 bars=26,940€/month, 100 bars=89,800€/month, 200 bars=179,600€/month (base: 20 spins/bar/evening, 20 evenings/month)

**3 Revenue streams:**
- Flux 1: SaaS subscriptions 89–249€/month
- Flux 2: Packs Fidélité + Roue (margin 70–80%) — not included in initial projections = conservative upside
- Flux 3: PvP commission 10% (100% margin); Tournament: 60/30/10% split

**Validated pricing grid (March 2026):**
- Starter: 89€/month (50 players)
- Standard: 149€/month (150 players)
- Premium: 249€/month (unlimited + multi-site)
- Annual = 2 months free (primary offer). Monthly = discovery option only.

**Beta scope:** QR code + blind test (Spotify/Deezer API) + bar culture quiz + manual Play coins + live leaderboard + 1-click dashboard. Onboarding: S1–2 Discovery → S3–4 Engagement (coins+PvP) → M2+ Complete (fidélité+Roue). Mode Gérant Pressé = 1 button. Health score per bar.

**Competitive benchmark (March 2026):**
- Amusoire: 159–279€, animation only, 600 establishments, no game economy
- Cotton Quiz: 69–149€, manager animates themselves, 150 venues, HPA overlap
- Buzzy Quizz: regional provider
- Hey Pongo: ~41€, CRM loyalty
- ZeroSix: digital loyalty
- Kadow: marketing gamification
- BarCoins: only platform combining animation + fidélité + economic gamification

**Founding Partners offer (3-phase):** 89€/month beta + 15 free days → -30% on first annual subscription only → lifetime price freeze at launch prices.

---

**Tools & resources**

- **Claude.ai:** Strategic brain (this project)
- **Claude Code:** Direct development on BarCoins codebase via WSL/Ubuntu terminal
- **OpenClaw:** Multi-agent system, localhost:18789, 6 agents (main, dev, legal, product, cfo, growth), Telegram bot jcduss35_bot
- **Storage:** OneDrive (`C:/Users/jean-/OneDrive/Documents/Barcoins/`) + Google Drive
- **PDF generation:** Python/ReportLab with DejaVu Sans TTF fonts (required for Unicode support — Helvetica drops special characters)
- **NotebookLM:** Podcast/audio generation from source documents
- **Gamma.app:** Pitch deck creation
- **Stripe:** Payment integration
- **Spotify/Deezer API:** Blind test audio (30s extracts, rights managed by provider, zero storage)
- **Qonto:** Business banking (to open)
- **Key financial institutions:** Initiative Pays Catalan, Créalia Occitanie, BPI France
